Seperated masses are implanted onto an aluminium

Measurement of rare gas activity during irradiation
is proportional to the cummulative yield of the
Measurment of the activity after the end of the
irradiation period is due only to decay of non-gaseous
precursors implanted in the aluminium foil.
Independant yields are normalised via the
corresponding cummulative yields. As many of these are
close to the mass chain yields, established data from
meek and rider, nedo-12154-1,1974, for chain yields
and fractional cummulative yields are used.
